| Greetings,
---
| With less than two weeks until Election Day, Team Scott is working hard to get out the vote, and make certain our strong grass roots support all across Vermont translates to a strong victory for Phil Scott on November 8. We know the economy and affordability must be the top priorities in Montpelier, and when we elect Phil Scott, they will be. Together, we know we can cross that finish line and send a clear message on the issues that matter to Vermonters! Check out this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign.

**HAVE YOU VOTED FOR PHIL YET?**
Early voting is open now and many predict we’ll have a record number of Vermonters choosing to vote early this year. Our early voting system means there is no reason to wait until election day to vote for Phil. Click [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=7b3b9f2ea8&e=19cebfe173) to find your town clerk's location or to request an absentee ballot, and get those votes in for Phil now. Then grab your friends, family and neighbors to do the same. We’re closing in on Election Day and we want to ensure everyone that supports Phil gets to the voting booth! **We can’t let up now!**

Voted early for Phil? Share your support with us on Twitter and Facebook: [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e85f3e5daf&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=752a5e853b&e=19cebfe173)
***
**PHIL’S ECONOMIC PLAN: AFFORDABLE HOUSING**
Phil’s comprehensive blueprint for economic growth, which you can view [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4675789792&e=19cebfe173), focuses on getting the state’s fiscal house in order, modernizing government and increasing efficiency, and setting clear budget goals and parameters. But, it also includes numerous ideas, proposals and initiatives that will make strategic, pro-growth investments to help make Vermont more affordable and retain and attract more working-age Vermonters and families. One such strategy is to **focus on affordable housing for all income levels**. Expanding our efforts beyond the important support we already provide low-income families is a particularly **important strategy for retaining and attracting young working families. We need to make housing affordable for those who make just a little too much to qualify for state assistance, and for middle-income families who struggle to find an affordable home that meets their needs**.
To **expand housing so that affordable options are available for more working families and young professionals** , Phil’s proposes we set aggressive goals for the construction and redevelopment of middle-income housing options; create a neighborhood designation for workforce housing that allows for funding, prioritization and building; and encourage redevelopment of existing housing through tax credits for small investors. Read more about these proposals [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=fe5ba6acbc&e=19cebfe173) (beginning on page 23).
Photo credit:@PhilScottVT

***
**ST. ALBANS MESSENGER & CALEDONIAN RECORD ENDORSE PHIL FOR GOVERNOR**

**The editorial boards of the _St. Albans Messenger_ and the _Caledonian Record_ have both endorsed Phil Scott for Governor**.

The _Messenger_ cites Phil’s intuitive knowledge of Vermont’s economic challenges, and the solutions he’s put forward to overcome them in its endorsement. You can read the full endorsement [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=fcf821afc5&e=19cebfe173), but here are a few highlights:

“...Mr. Scott’s strength is that **he intuitively understands Vermont is over-burdened at many levels** , and that we need some time to do well what we have already been tasked to do...”

“...We are at the cusp of **a new debate on how the state deals with child care** and how it intersects with the state’s need for economic development. **This is a critical issue that fits hand in glove with Mr. Scott’s push for a broad economic development platform**. We need to be focused on tomorrow’s workforce needs...”

“Mr. Scott’s business background versus Ms. Minter’s 24-year career in state government is also appealing. He knows about limits. And **he knows the value of limits and how they can force conversations** in nonlinear ways. **Getting from here to there _isn’t always about spending more_ , it’s about doing things differently**, something you learn running a construction business that ebbs and flows with the economic times.”

The _Record’s_ endorsement asserts, “Phil Scott is his own man. He clearly sees that **Vermont has to move in a different direction, and he has the experience and grit to take us there**.” Read the entire endorsement [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=6aa887df9c&e=19cebfe173), and see more highlights below.

“Scott says his top priorities are economic growth, jobs, fiscal conservatism, and making Vermont affordable for ordinary families. **He has promised to _restrain state spending_ to the rate of inflation, _abandon Gov. Shumlin’s failed health insurance exchange_ , defend and promote parental _choice in education_ , and do _battle against the tax raising urges_ of a (likely) Democratic legislature**.”

“Phil Scott describes himself, accurately we believe, as **a ‘moderate, frugal, common sense Vermonter**.’ He has been a medium-sized businessman and understands the challenge of meeting a payroll. He almost always **leans against costly new spending programs and the new taxes needed to pay for them**. Above all, Scott clearly sees the **need to _stop making Vermonters into laboratory rats_ for every new liberal experiment in bigger, more costly government**.”
*******
**PHIL CONTINUES TO BUILD CROSS-PARTY SUPPORT**
Last week we introduced [a new ad](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=5bf9c3466d&e=19cebfe173) showing a handful of **the many Vermont Democrats who plan to vote for Phil for Governor, including Sen. Dick Mazza (D-Grand Isle)**. That support has continued to grow as Vermonters learn more about Phil’s commitment to making Vermont more affordable, and his plans to end our unsustainable budget practices and grow our economy.

In a new web video, Shelby, a teacher from Northfield, shares more on why she, a Democrat, is voting for Phil. “I know **we can trust Phil Scott** **on the issues that mean the most to our family** ...**He has a plan to strengthen the economy, create good jobs and grow the middle class** ,” she says, describing why she’ll vote for Phil. View the full video [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=d597a2766a&e=19cebfe173). Adding to that public support, Michael Crane of Burlington wrote in the Burlington Free Press, “**I am a Democrat voting for Phil Scott** this year and I encourage my friends to do the same.” Read his Letter to the Editor [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=440126a52a&e=19cebfe173).
---
| [ ](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=dd83e4a59d&e=19cebfe173)
---
Phil Scott for Vermont: Trust
|
*******
**FACT CHECKING AHEAD!**
After more **strong debate performances and clear victories for Phil** , and following a [WCAX poll](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=48684dd17e&e=19cebfe173) that **Phil has a solid lead in the race for Governor** , his opponent’s desperation to distract reached a new level. We want to make certain Vermonters know the truth, as that desperation turns into misleading attacks against Phil and flip-flopping on her positions. First, in Wednesday’s debate, **his opponent yet again misleads voters on her position on a carbon tax. Check out our fact check**[**HERE**](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=21af69f832&e=19cebfe173)**. In summary, she supports a broad regional carbon tax**.

Next, we started getting calls from Democratic supporters from throughout Vermont reporting a “survey” that was testing a range of dishonest statements and outright lies about Phil Scott, his positions, his plans and his record. **We debunk those claims**[**HERE**](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=0402ffad37&e=19cebfe173)**.**

And most egregiously, **a PAC supporting Phil’s opponent is running ads that completely distort Phil’s record as a pro-choice candidate**. This week, a former Planned Parenthood board member and “volunteer of the year” recipient **sets the record straight on Phil’s support of a women’s right to choose.** Watch that video [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=2863b998c1&e=19cebfe173).

**Phil’s working hard to make this a campaign about the issues that matter to Vermonters: the economy and affordability**. It’s unfortunate we keep having to set the record straight on blatant falsehoods!
*******
**TEAM SCOTT GETS INTO THE FALL SPIRIT**
It’s one of the greatest times of year to be a Vermonter: Fall! Team Scott got in the true Fall spirit this week, attending a Haunted House in **Pittsford** , attending a class at **UVM** , tasting some fall brews at **Hop’n Moose Brewing Company in Rutland** , and checking out the snow in **Killington**. That’s right, we said snow! We also enjoyed attending the **Shear Heaven’s Combat Cancer event** , and meeting with Vermonters at the **Lake Champlain Regional Chamber** , **Williston Rotary Club** , and the **Vermont Business for Social Responsibility** ’s candidate showcase.

Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT
Stay up-to-date on our upcoming events [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=2be76af1fd&e=19cebfe173).
***
**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

**_WPTZ debate shows clear choice: Phil will not increase the tax burden on Vermonters_**
“I believe that [a carbon tax] will just ratchet up the cost of living in Vermont. I don’t think we can afford it. I’m not looking to do anything that would raise the cost of living on already-struggling Vermonters.” (Source: WPTZ: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9a9c74ecb1&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil outlines plans as Governor_**
“We need to start living within our means with a state budget that takes care of the most vulnerable, our public safety needs, but also focuses on growing areas where we can revitalize our economy.” (Source: Bennington Banner: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=09940ef10c&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil on the latest reports of government waste and lack of oversight_**
"[Vermonters] deserve a state government that uses proven management strategies to oversee programs and measure success. They deserve a state government that is a good steward of their resources. As Governor, my administration will implement these procedures, and restore much-needed faith and trust in state government.” (Source: VT Digger: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=0842a75a73&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil Scott for a stronger Vermont_**
“Phil was always more interested in getting things done than getting credit. He always supported the best ideas and solutions, regardless of who they came from or what party they were in. If it was sensible, he was all for it.” – Dawn Terrill, former Secretary of Transportation (Source: Caledonian Record: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=6bf788526e&e=19cebfe173))

**_Scott beats Minter on the Issues_**
“Phil Scott will move us off VHC, which has cost $200+ million in implementation costs (plus another $50+ million annually in operating costs) and still doesn’t work...Scott will not raise taxes on already tapped-out Vermonters...Scott will limit the growth of the state budget to economic growth to ensure state government lives within its means. Scott released a comprehensive, ten-point opiate plan which covers prevention, treatment, and enforcement. It will expand the number of counselors and treatment options, and build on community approaches such as Rutland’s Project VISION.” – Colette Dublois, Manchester Center (Source: Caledonian Record: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=30c837474b&e=19cebfe173))
***
**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**

**_Art Woolf: Vermont among top spenders on education_**
Analyzing new data from the Census Bureau and U.S. Department of Education, UVM Associate Professor of Economics Art Woolf sheds light on the high cost of Vermont’s current education system. This data shows that **Vermont is fifth highest in the nation when it comes to per student spending** and we’re “members of an exclusive club when it comes to our high level of education spending.” Looking at the justification for high spending in the four states that rank higher than Vermont – New York, New Jersey, Alaska and Connecticut – Woolf notes that Vermont doesn’t have the same justifications as those top four spenders. “**We just spend a lot in education.** ” With **no clear measure of how that spending impacts outcomes** , Woolf suggests the state take a good hard look at whether or not we could fund our education system more efficiently.

**Phil is the only candidate who’s been willing to have _an honest discussion_ about our education spending**. And he’s the only candidate to acknowledge that **we can provide the best education in the country, while reducing the property tax burden**. But, we have to be willing to **modernize how we’re administering our $1.6 billion education budget for 86,000 children**. Read Prof. Woolf’s full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=2c6af810ac&e=19cebfe173).

**_Keith Ballek: A Democrat’s View on Democrats_**
As we’ve shared over the last few weeks, many Democrats are crossing the aisle to vote for Phil this year, or are just overall fed up with the direction the state has been going. Keith Ballek, a delegate to the Vermont Democratic Committee and chair of the Sheffield Democratic Committee discussed some his frustrations in VT Digger this week. “Unfortunately, there has been what can only be described as a corporate capture of a number of [Democratic] party leaders in the Legislature and the governorship. **This culture of deeply entrenched corporate interests and their lobbyists are already clamoring around Sue Minter, planning to ramp up their influence even further if she is elected** ,” Ballek writes. “Some independent thinking and voting one’s conscience regardless of party affiliation, checks and balances in Montpelier, and **a break from one-party rule is needed** along with forward-thinking discussion and policy making. Otherwise the downward spiral will continue,” he concludes. Read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=cf911f83be&e=19cebfe173).
*******
Thanks to everyone for continuing to follow and support our campaign. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities every day – and share your thoughts – [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=088b4ae809&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=0791789089&e=19cebfe173).

Sincerely,
Team Scott
---

Dick Mazza (D-Grand Isle). That support has continued to grow as Vermonters learn more about Phil’s commitment to making Vermont more affordable, and his plans to end our unsustainable budget practices and grow our economy. In a new web video, Shelby, a teacher from Northfield, shares more on why she, a Democrat, is voting for Phil. “I know we can trust Phil Scott on the issues that mean the most to our family ...He has a plan to strengthen the economy, create good jobs and grow the middle class ,” she says, describing why she’ll vote for Phil. View the full video . Adding to that public support, Michael Crane of Burlington wrote in the Burlington Free Press, “I am a Democrat voting for Phil Scott this year and I encourage my friends to do the same.” Read his Letter to the Editor . | Phil Scott for Vermont: Trust | FACT CHECKING AHEAD! After more strong debate performances and clear victories for Phil , and following a that Phil has a solid lead in the race for Governor , his opponent’s desperation to distract reached a new level. We want to make certain Vermonters know the truth, as that desperation turns into misleading attacks against Phil and flip-flopping on her positions. First, in Wednesday’s debate, his opponent yet again misleads voters on her position on a carbon tax. Check out our fact check. In summary, she supports a broad regional carbon tax. Next, we started getting calls from Democratic supporters from throughout Vermont reporting a “survey” that was testing a range of dishonest statements and outright lies about Phil Scott, his positions, his plans and his record. We debunk those claims. And most egregiously, a PAC supporting Phil’s opponent is running ads that completely distort Phil’s record as a pro-choice candidate. This week, a former Planned Parenthood board member and “volunteer of the year” recipient sets the record straight on Phil’s support of a women’s right to choose. Watch that video . Phil’s working hard to make this a campaign about the issues that matter to Vermonters: the economy and affordability. It’s unfortunate we keep having to set the record straight on blatant falsehoods! TEAM SCOTT GETS INTO THE FALL SPIRIT It’s one of the greatest times of year to be a Vermonter: Fall! Team Scott got in the true Fall spirit this week, attending a Haunted House in Pittsford , attending a class at UVM , tasting some fall brews at Hop’n Moose Brewing Company in Rutland , and checking out the snow in Killington. That’s right, we said snow! We also enjoyed attending the Shear Heaven’s Combat Cancer event , and meeting with Vermonters at the Lake Champlain Regional Chamber , Williston Rotary Club , and the Vermont Business for Social Responsibility ’s candidate showcase. Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT Stay up-to-date on our upcoming events . ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS WPTZ debate shows clear choice: Phil will not increase the tax burden on Vermonters “I believe that [a carbon tax] will just ratchet up the cost of living in Vermont. I don’t think we can afford it. I’m not looking to do anything that would raise the cost of living on already-struggling Vermonters.” (Source: WPTZ: ) Phil outlines plans as Governor “We need to start living within our means with a state budget that takes care of the most vulnerable, our public safety needs, but also focuses on growing areas where we can revitalize our economy.” (Source: Bennington Banner: ) Phil on the latest reports of government waste and lack of oversight "[Vermonters] deserve a state government that uses proven management strategies to oversee programs and measure success. They deserve a state government that is a good steward of their resources. As Governor, my administration will implement these procedures, and restore much-needed faith and trust in state government.” (Source: VT Digger: ) Phil Scott for a stronger Vermont “Phil was always more interested in getting things done than getting credit. He always supported the best ideas and solutions, regardless of who they came from or what party they were in. If it was sensible, he was all for it.” – Dawn Terrill, former Secretary of Transportation (Source: Caledonian Record: ) Scott beats Minter on the Issues “Phil Scott will move us off VHC, which has cost $200+ million in implementation costs (plus another $50+ million annually in operating costs) and still doesn’t work...Scott will not raise taxes on already tapped-out Vermonters...Scott will limit the growth of the state budget to economic growth to ensure state government lives within its means. Scott released a comprehensive, ten-point opiate plan which covers prevention, treatment, and enforcement. It will expand the number of counselors and treatment options, and build on community approaches such as Rutland’s Project VISION.” – Colette Dublois, Manchester Center (Source: Caledonian Record: ) ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS Art Woolf: Vermont among top spenders on education Analyzing new data from the Census Bureau and U.S. Department of Education, UVM Associate Professor of Economics Art Woolf sheds light on the high cost of Vermont’s current education system. This data shows that Vermont is fifth highest in the nation when it comes to per student spending and we’re “members of an exclusive club when it comes to our high level of education spending.” Looking at the justification for high spending in the four states that rank higher than Vermont – New York, New Jersey, Alaska and Connecticut – Woolf notes that Vermont doesn’t have the same justifications as those top four spenders. “We just spend a lot in education. ” With no clear measure of how that spending impacts outcomes , Woolf suggests the state take a good hard look at whether or not we could fund our education system more efficiently. Phil is the only candidate who’s been willing to have an honest discussion about our education spending. And he’s the only candidate to acknowledge that we can provide the best education in the country, while reducing the property tax burden. But, we have to be willing to modernize how we’re administering our $1.6 billion education budget for 86,000 children. Read Prof. Woolf’s full article . Keith Ballek: A Democrat’s View on Democrats As we’ve shared over the last few weeks, many Democrats are crossing the aisle to vote for Phil this year, or are just overall fed up with the direction the state has been going. Keith Ballek, a delegate to the Vermont Democratic Committee and chair of the Sheffield Democratic Committee discussed some his frustrations in VT Digger this week. “Unfortunately, there has been what can only be described as a corporate capture of a number of [Democratic] party leaders in the Legislature and the governorship. This culture of deeply entrenched corporate interests and their lobbyists are already clamoring around Sue Minter, planning to ramp up their influence even further if she is elected ,” Ballek writes. “Some independent thinking and voting one’s conscience regardless of party affiliation, checks and balances in Montpelier, and a break from one-party rule is needed along with forward-thinking discussion and policy making. Otherwise the downward spiral will continue,” he concludes. Read the full article . Thanks to everyone for continuing to follow and support our campaign. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities every day – and share your thoughts – and . Sincerely, Team Scott | "DONATE TODAY!" | "JOIN THE TEAM!" | | | | | | | | | | | | Phil Scott for Governor, Glen Wright, Treasurer, 46 Three Mile Bridge Road, Middlesex, VT 05602 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can or

body:
Every dollar counts because we’re going to place it all into our urgent advertising programs. |

Friend, Can I tell my father that you’re in the fight with us? With less than two weeks left until Election Day, our movement is gathering hundreds of supporters by the minute who want to take their country back. Are you one of these American patriots who’s tired of politics as usual and wants to turn this country around? If you are, then my father needs your support to make this happen. And to show you how much he values your support, he is going to add to your donation by putting in $10 million of his own money to help fund the final ad blitz of the campaign. CONTRIBUTE $35 CONTRIBUTE $50 CONTRIBUTE $100 Friend, once my father gets elected, he’s going to make a lot of big changes in this country. He’s going to stop the revolving door of illegal immigration by building a wall, making Mexico pay for it, and making our country’s borders secure again. He’s going to repeal and replace ObamaCare that has only caused you to have less control over your health care decisions and pay more and more in premiums each year. Friend, my father’s first 100 days in office are going to be spent changing our country for the better. But for him to get there, he needs your support. And remember, every dollar counts because we’re going to place it all into our urgent advertising programs. Donate $35, $50, or $100 to guarantee a Trump presidency. In less than 2 weeks, this election will be over, but my father’s fight to change America for the better will only just be starting. Make sure to give my father a fighting chance to make our country great again by donating $35, $50, or $100 right now. Thank you, Donald Trump Jr. CONTRIBUTE $100

body:
Remember when Republicans said more dark money was coming after plowing $25 million into Senate toss-ups? Well, we just heard... [****](https://secure.dscc. ---
_**CNN: MCCONNELL SUPER PAC RAISES $43 MILLION TO HELP GOP SENATORS**_
[**We 've never needed you more! Pitch in a TRIPLE-MATCHED gift NOW >>**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4w?refcode=DPE-20161028-DevelopingDS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&recur_weekly=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

---
Did you see the news this week, Friend?

1\. _**WEDNESDAY**_ : Mitch McConnell's favorite Super PAC announced a $43 MILLION dark money haul ... thanks to **" several multi-million dollar checks"!**

2\. _**THURSDAY**_ : Republicans immediately dumped $25 MILLION into SIX Senate toss-ups!

3\. _**SO TODAY**_ : We announced a **[TRIPLE-MATCH on all gifts](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4w?refcode=DPE-20161028-DevelopingDS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&recur_weekly=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5") **until Monday's crucial end-of-month deadline -- our best chance to fight back this FLOOD of dark money!
[**Friend, we can 't win back the Senate without the full support of grassroots Democrats like you. Will you pitch in while the TRIPLE-MATCH lasts to MAXIMIZE your impact?**](https://secure.dscc. subject: Press Release: Bill Bryant Closes Inslee's Lead to Just 4%
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Washingtonians for Bryant (R)
body:
| | |
---

| |
---
| | For Immediate Release
Contact: Jason Roe (253)220-5051
Jason@BillBryantForGovernor.com
**ICYMI: Bill Bryant Closes Inslee's Lead to Just 4%**
_University of Washington Poll Shows Race for Governor a Toss-Up_
(Seattle, Wash.) - Yesterday [a poll](http://billbryantforgovernor.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a508ffc703e6fc82360a383e8&id=a5239b42e8&e=32f1a3df98) released from the University of Washington/KCTS/Crosscut shows the race for governor tightening up as Election Day approaches. The poll put incumbent Jay Inslee at 46% and Bill Bryant at 42%, among decided voters. With just 11 days to go, an incumbent so far under 50%, a challenger on his heels, and a trend toward Bryant points to an Election Day photo finish.

“Voters are starting to pay attention to Governor Inslee’s record of failure and they're seeing a better alternative,” said Bryant. “This poll shows what we’ve said all along, that once voters hear our message of reducing traffic, addressing homelessness, fixing school funding, and creating jobs, they become Bill Bryant voters. We have some work to do to get those undecided and leaning voters to our side, but with 11 days left we’re confident our hard work will pay off.”

To see the poll summary and find a link to the full poll visit **[www.billbryantforgovernor.com/uwpoll](http://billbryantforgovernor.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a508ffc703e6fc82360a383e8&id=30b743fccc&e=32f1a3df98)**
###
